title: Pulp project-import IR v0
description: Versioned, framework-AGNOSTIC intermediate representation produced by a project importer (an add-on tool, installed on demand) when it reads an existing audio-plugin project read-only and proposes a Pulp migration scaffold. This is the contract between the Pulp SDK (which owns emission) and the importer tool (which owns framework-specific parsing). The SDK never hardcodes a source-framework identity: `source.framework` and `vendor_id` are runtime DATA strings, not enumerated values, so the SDK, mainline tests, and CI carry no vendor names. This IR is project-shaped and is deliberately distinct from the design-import `ImportDiagnostic` / source-contract (core/view + tools/import-validation), which is design-shaped; the two must not be conflated or merged. The schema is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties true on open-ended objects) so importers may carry framework-specific extras without a schema bump, while the required fields lock the facts every consumer relies on. Honesty is structural: a fact the importer could not statically resolve is recorded as a low-confidence value plus a first-class `constructs[]` entry and a diagnostic, never silently dropped or guessed.

schema.description: Schema tag, e.g. 'pulp.import.project_ir.v0'. Consumers must check this before reading.

parameters.description: Statically resolved parameters. Parameters the importer could NOT enumerate (loops, factories, computed args) live in `constructs`, not here.

constructs.description: First-class inventory of parameter (or other) constructs the importer found but could not reduce to concrete facts. Lets emission report 'N unresolved constructs of unknown cardinality' instead of under-reporting a fabricated count.

migration_tasks.description: Human-facing TODOs the residual report surfaces.

confidence.description: Per-fact confidence in [0,1]. Consumers should report per-section confidence and a weighted overall, not a single global minimum.

source_ref.description: A location in the user's source tree. Read-only provenance for a fact.

file.description: Path relative to the source project root.

macro_expansion.description: True when the spelling location differs from the expansion location (the fact came through a macro).

extraction_context.description: How this IR was extracted, so VST3-vs-AU / debug-vs-release / macro-conditional runs are explainable and the parse environment is auditable. The shipped importer records the full compile command, macro set, include graph, target triple, and exact parser/libclang version.

tool.description: Importer tool id (framework-agnostic at the SDK layer).

parser_version.description: Exact parser identity; pinned + reported so behaviour drift is explainable.

target.description: Target triple or a documented placeholder.

compile_db.description: How compile flags were obtained: a real compile_commands.json, a generated one, or a documented fallback. Guessing include flags is a hard boundary, not a silent fallback — record which path was used.

system_includes.description: How the system/SDK include search paths were resolved.

framework_resolution.description: How framework #includes were resolved (the user's own checkout path at the SDK boundary; never vendored).

source.description: Identity of the imported project. `framework`/`vendor_id` are runtime data strings — the SDK never enumerates them.

framework.description: Runtime framework id (DATA, not an SDK enum).

metadata_source.description: Where metadata came from (project manifest / build script / none).

metadata.description: Maps onto pulp::format::PluginDescriptor. `pulp_category` is one of the three Pulp categories; `category` is the importer's own framework-shaped label (data).

param_range.description: LINEAR range mapping onto pulp::state::ParamRange. Source non-linearity is recorded separately in `source_curve`.

source_curve.description: Source parameter curve recorded separately because Pulp's registered ParamInfo.range is linear today. A non-unity skew flags the emitted param PARTIAL until SDK shaped-range lands.

parameter.description: Maps onto pulp::state::ParamInfo. Identity is preserved as the source string id + version hint AND a stable proposed uint32 id so preset/state migration survives id assignment.

proposed_pulp_id.description: Stable uint32 hash of the source id (Pulp ParamID is uint32).

display_conversion.description: Whether display string conversion is the framework default or a custom (non-data-extractable) callback that must be migrated by hand.

computed.description: True if any constructor argument depends on a runtime value; such params also appear in `constructs`.

construct.description: A parameter (or similar) construct the importer found but could not reduce to concrete facts (loop / factory / computed args / nested group). Carries cardinality bounds so consumers report unresolved counts honestly.

bus.description: Maps onto pulp::format::BusInfo. `optional` is true for buses not activated by default (e.g. a sidechain). Order is declaration order: main bus at index 0.

channels.description: Channel count (channel-set expressions are resolved to a count).

source.description: Whether buses were declared explicitly or derived from category (lower confidence). Dynamic layout policy is flagged, not imported.

reachability_scope.description: How deep the DSP reachability analysis went. portable-core confidence must not be read from a shallow direct_body scan.

format_matrix.description: Per-format differences (formats enabled, per-format defines, bus/MIDI/category deltas behind conditional compilation). Lets a consumer see why two formats extracted different worlds.

project_inputs.description: Provenance of the files read: project manifest(s), build scripts, enabled framework modules, framework version/path, generated-file roots. Expensive to reconstruct later; critical for the residual report.

residual_policy.description: Whether emission is complete, partial, or blocked, with reason codes — feeds the migration_status manifest and the `pulp ship` unresolved-scaffold gate.

diagnostic.description: Project-import diagnostic (distinct from the design-import ImportDiagnostic). Neutral language; never accusatory.
